channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: MIT rejects Trump admin funding compact, citing free expression concerns
item.description: The Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) rejected a Trump administration proposal that would usher in sweeping reforms in exchange for benefits.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A sacred lake, a Columbus statue, and an Indigenous people's long struggle for land
item.description: GEDDES, N.Y. (AP) — Before white settlers came to Onondaga Lake, before the city of Syracuse grew along its shore, before the pristine waters became one of the most polluted bodies of water in the United States, it was a sacred place for the Onondaga Nation.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyers seek dismissal of federal charges in assassination of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lawyers for Luigi Mangione asked a New York federal judge Saturday to dismiss some criminal charges, including the only count for which he could face the death penalty, from a federal indictment brought against him in the December assassination of UnitedHealthcare’s chief executive.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israel rejects freeing from prison the most popular Palestinian leader
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — The most popular and potentially unifying Palestinian leader — Marwan Barghouti — is not among the prisoners Israel intends to free in exchange for hostages held by Hamas under the new Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH, JULIA FRANKEL and JALAL BWAITAL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Officials investigate blast at Tennessee explosives plant that left 18 missing and feared dead
item.description: Officials are investigating a blast that leveled an explosives plant in rural Tennessee, as families of the 18 people missing and feared dead wait anxiously for answers.
item.dc:creator: Travis Loller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A long-lost ancient Roman artifact reappears in a New Orleans backyard
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A New Orleans family cleaning up their overgrown backyard made an extremely unusual find: Under the weeds was a mysterious marble tablet with Latin characters that included the phrase “spirits of the dead.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Orleans is electing a new mayor and a wide field is pledging change after indictments
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans voters on Saturday are picking a new mayor to replace two-term incumbent LaToya Cantrell, whose indictment on corruption charges has left a wide field of candidates promising change to city hall.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blast at a Tennessee explosives plant leaves 18 missing and feared dead, sheriff says
item.description: McEWEN, Tenn. (AP) — A blast leveled an explosives plant Friday in rural Tennessee, leaving behind a mass of twisted metal, burned-out shells of cars and at least 18 people missing and feared dead, authorities said. “There’s nothing to describe.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OSHA violations reported prior to massive explosion at Tennessee facility: documents
item.description: As investigators continue to comb through the wreckage of an explosion in Tennessee that left 18 people unaccounted for Friday, Nexstar's WKRN has learned more about the company that operated the facility.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Wilson, Fox 5

item.title: President Donald Trump is in 'exceptional health,' his doctor says, after visit to Walter Reed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is in “exceptional health," his physician said Friday after he underwent a checkup that included lab tests and preventive health assessments at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: OSHA violations reported prior to massive explosion at Tennessee facility: documents
item.description: As investigators continue to comb through the wreckage of an explosion in Tennessee that left 18 people unaccounted for Friday, Nexstar's WKRN has learned more about the company that operated the facility.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Wilson, KRON4

item.title: Two jurors claim they were bullied into convicting Harvey Weinstein and regret it, his lawyers say
item.description: Two jurors who voted in June to convict Harvey Weinstein of sexual assault said they regret the decision and only did so because others on the panel bullied them, the former movie mogul’s lawyers said in a newly public court filing.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's quest for the Nobel Peace Prize falls short again despite high-profile nominations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump was passed over for the Nobel Peace Prize on Friday despite jockeying from his fellow Republicans, various world leaders and — most vocally — himself.
item.dc:creator: MIKE PESOLI and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AstraZeneca agrees to lower drug prices for Medicaid under Trump administration deal
item.description: AstraZeneca on Friday became the second major pharmaceutical manufacturer to announce it had agreed to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Medicaid under a deal struck with the Trump administration that avoided its threats of steep tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump threatens tech export limits, new 100% tariff on Chinese imports starting Nov. 1 or sooner
item.description: Trump said that “starting November 1st, 2025 (or sooner, depending on any further actions or changes taken by China), the United States of America will impose a Tariff of 100% on China, over and above any Tariff that they are currently paying.
item.dc:creator: KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scrapple sculpture contest winner prevailed in Eagles country by putting the pig into pigskin
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — In the contest for best scrapple sculpture in Philadelphia on Friday it was the “Mush Push” fashioned from the regionally popular pork breakfast meat that got Patrick Moser's entry over the goal line.
item.dc:creator: TASSANEE VEJPONGSA and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AstraZeneca agrees to lower drug prices for Medicaid under Trump administration deal
item.description: AstraZeneca on Friday became the second major pharmaceutical manufacturer to announce it had agreed to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Medicaid under a deal struck with the Trump administration that avoided its threats of steep tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Troops will miss paychecks next week without action on the government shutdown
item.description: The nation’s third shutdown in 12 years is once again raising anxiety levels among service members and their families because those in uniform are working without pay.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, beloved chaplain at Loyola University Chicago, dies at 106
item.description: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, a beloved member of the Loyola University Chicago community who was well-known as the chaplain to the Loyola University men's basketball team, died Thursday at the age of 106, according to the university.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, KRON4

item.title: Nor'easter without a name to bring ocean flooding and high winds to US East Coast
item.description: Tropical weather in the Atlantic and Pacific oceans is causing dangerous conditions in the United States, Mexico and the Caribbean.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tesla self-driving software under investigation after traffic violations
item.description: The NHTSA pointed to 18 complaints and one media report in which Tesla vehicles using the self-driving software failed to remain stopped or to stop fully at a red light or failed to accurately detect the traffic signal.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, ABC 4

item.title: Senate passes mammoth annual defense policy bill
item.description: The Senate approved its massive annual defense policy bill late Thursday as the U.S. government remains shut down. The GOP-led chamber approved the National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) 70-20, aiming to fund the U.S. military at $924.7 billion in fiscal 2026.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas Jewish community expresses hope, hesitation on peace deal between Israel, Hamas
item.description: Israel and Hamas are taking a major step toward ending their two-year-long war. The new deal calls for Hamas to release all hostages and for Israel to pull back its forces from Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Jezzamine Wolk, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tennessee teacher fired over Charlie Kirk posts files free speech lawsuit
item.description: A longtime Rutherford County teacher is suing her school district, claiming she was fired for what she shared online.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Milan, ABC 4

item.title: Saying 'I do' to AI? Ohio lawmaker proposes ban on marriage, legal personhood for AI
item.description: “As the computer systems improve in their capacity to act more like humans, we want to be sure we have prohibitions in our law that prohibit those systems from ever being human in their agency.
item.dc:creator: Ava Boldizar, NewsNation

item.title: Drake's defamation lawsuit against label over Kendrick Lamar's 'Not Like Us' tossed out
item.description: In his lawsuit Drake asserted that the song amounts to “falsely accusing him of being a sex offender, engaging in pedophilic acts” and more.
item.dc:creator: Larry Newumeister, KRON4

item.title: Taco Bell, KFC franchisee using AI to improve customer experience
item.description: Rather than displacing employees, as some fear AI might, Coach AI can serve as "a tool to help the worker.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Lyft scraps feature that let drivers see how often riders tip
item.description: Lyft has scrapped a test feature that showed some drivers how often passengers tip before accepting a ride.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Arizona sheriff's office misused millions meant to remedy racial profiling, report reveals
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The sheriff’s office for metro Phoenix spent millions of dollars budgeted for compliance costs in a racial profiling case over Joe Arpaio’s immigration crackdowns on things that had little or nothing to do with a court-ordered overhaul of the agency, according to an expert’s report.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alex Jones asks Supreme Court to halt $1.4 billion defamation judgment
item.description: Conspiracy theorist Alex Jones urged the Supreme Court to immediately halt a $1.44 billion defamation judgment he owes for falsely claiming the Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting was a hoax.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Did Trump end 7 wars? What to know ahead of Nobel Peace Prize announcement
item.description: President Trump's moment of truth as a global peacemaker will come early Friday morning when the Nobel Peace Prize is awarded. Trump has made no secret of his desire to win the prize.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Pack of coyotes kill leashed dog, injure woman in California
item.description: Neighbors in one Solana Beach neighborhood are airing concerns over coyotes in their neighborhood after 82-year old, Roberta Hennigan, was injured and her dog, Daisy, killed when a pack of coyotes attacked.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Vara, KRON4

item.title: US diplomat fired for romantic relationship with woman with ties to Chinese Communist Party
item.description: The State Department said it fired a U.S. diplomat who concealed his relationship with a woman who had known ties to the Chinese Communist Party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Halloween display often mistaken for burning home
item.description: FOUNTAIN INN, S.C. (WSPA) -- A Halloween display in Fountain Inn is captivating locals with its elaborate decorations, featuring lights and smoke machines.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Indiana man convicted in 2001 rape and murder of teenager to be executed by lethal injection
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — An Indiana man convicted in the 2001 rape and murder of a teenage girl was set to die by lethal injection early Friday in the state’s third execution since resuming capital punishment last year.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Prosecutors say no harm was done by social media posts about assassination of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal prosecutors told a judge Wednesday that no harm was done to prospects for a fair trial after two U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How American views on Israel and antisemitism have changed since Oct. 7
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Nearly two years after the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks in Israel and the country’s ensuing invasion of the Gaza Strip, Americans are more divided on the Israeli-Palestinian conflict than they were before the war – and concerns are mounting about the safety of Jewish communities at home.
item.dc:creator: MAYA SWEEDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4
